request.description:
## Register Tenant

Registers a new tenant in the system by accepting basic personal information and returning the created tenant record.

---

## Request Details

| Property | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Method** | `POST` |
| **URL** | `http://localhost:3000/tenants/register` |

---

## Request Body Parameters

The request body must be sent as JSON (`application/json`).

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `first_name` | `string` | ✅ Yes | The first name of the tenant |
| `last_name` | `string` | ✅ Yes | The last name of the tenant |
| `email` | `string` | ✅ Yes | The email address of the tenant (used as a unique identifier) |

---

## Example Request Body

``` json
{
  "first_name": "rahul",
  "last_name": "ranjan",
  "email": "rahul@gmail.com"
}

 ```

---

## Example Response

**Status:** `201 Created`

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Tenant registered successfully",
  "data": {
    "createdAt": "2026-04-25T11:41:24.035Z",
    "updatedAt": "2026-04-25T11:41:24.035Z",
    "id": 2,
    "first_name": "rahul",
    "last_name": "ranjan",
    "email": "rahul@gmail.com"
  }
}

 ```

---

## Response Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `success` | `boolean` | Indicates whether the registration was successful |
| `message` | `string` | A human-readable message describing the result |
| `data.id` | `integer` | The unique identifier assigned to the newly created tenant |
| `data.first_name` | `string` | The first name of the registered tenant |
| `data.last_name` | `string` | The last name of the registered tenant |
| `data.email` | `string` | The email address of the registered tenant |
| `data.createdAt` | `string (ISO 8601)` | Timestamp of when the tenant record was created |
| `data.updatedAt` | `string (ISO 8601)` | Timestamp of when the tenant record was last updated |

Retrieves a list of all registered tenants along with their associated documents.

## Request

**Method:** GET  
**URL:** `http://localhost:3000/tenants/getAll`

No request parameters, headers, or body required.

## Response

Returns a JSON object with the following structure:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `success` | boolean | Indicates whether the request was successful |
| `data` | array | List of tenant objects |

### Tenant Object

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | integer | Unique identifier for the tenant |
| `first_name` | string | Tenant's first name |
| `last_name` | string | Tenant's last name |
| `email` | string | Tenant's email address |
| `createdAt` | string (ISO 8601) | Timestamp when the tenant was created |
| `updatedAt` | string (ISO 8601) | Timestamp when the tenant was last updated |
| `documents` | array | List of documents associated with the tenant |

### Document Object

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | integer | Unique identifier for the document |
| `file_name` | string | Name of the document file |
| `file_size` | integer|null | Size of the file in bytes (null if unknown) |
| `file_type` | string | MIME type or file type of the document |
| `tenant_id` | integer | ID of the tenant this document belongs to |
| `createdAt` | string (ISO 8601) | Timestamp when the document was created |
| `updatedAt` | string (ISO 8601) | Timestamp when the document was last updated |

## Example Response

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "first_name": "rahul",
      "last_name": "ranjan",
      "email": "rahul@gmail.com",
      "createdAt": "2026-04-24T23:28:45.509Z",
      "updatedAt": "2026-04-24T23:28:45.510Z",
      "documents": [
        {
          "id": 1,
          "file_name": "doc12",
          "file_size": null,
          "file_type": "unknown",
          "tenant_id": 1,
          "createdAt": "2026-04-24T23:28:56.845Z",
          "updatedAt": "2026-04-24T23:28:56.845Z"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

 ```

Search documents for a specific tenant using Elasticsearch.

This endpoint queries the Elasticsearch index to retrieve documents belonging to a given tenant. Results can be filtered by a search query string or a specific document ID.

**Query Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `tenant_id` | integer | Yes | The ID of the tenant whose documents should be searched. |
| `q` | string | No | A search query string to filter documents by name. It uses **fuzzy search**. |
| `id` | integer | No | Filter results by a specific document ID. |
| `file_name` | string | No | Filter result by accurate file name. This is fast as compare to q parameter. |

**Response:**

Returns a JSON object with a `data` field containing:

- `success` (boolean): Whether the search was successful.
    
- `total` (integer): Total number of matching documents.
    
- `data` (array): List of matching document objects, each containing:
    
    - `id`: Document ID.
        
    - `file_name`: Name of the file.
        
    - `file_size`: Size of the file (nullable).
        
    - `file_type`: Type/format of the file.
        
    - `tenant_id`: The tenant this document belongs to.
        
    - `createdAt` / `updatedAt`: Timestamps.

## Create Document

Creates a new document record associated with a specific tenant.

---

### Request Body

The request body must be sent as raw JSON (`application/json`).

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `file_name` | string | ✅ Yes | The name of the document/file to be created. |
| `tenant_id` | string | ✅ Yes | The ID of the tenant to associate this document with. |

**Example Request Body:**

``` json
{
  "file_name": "doc21",
  "tenant_id": "2"
}

 ```

---

### Response

#### ✅ 201 Created

Returned when the document is successfully created.

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `success` | boolean | Indicates whether the request was successful. |
| `message` | string | A human-readable message confirming the operation. |
| `data` | object | The newly created document object. |
| `data.id` | integer | Unique identifier for the created document. |
| `data.file_name` | string | The name of the document as provided in the request. |
| `data.file_size` | integer | null | The size of the file in bytes. `null` if not yet determined. |
| `data.file_type` | string | The type/format of the file. Defaults to `"unknown"` if not detected. |
| `data.tenant_id` | integer | The ID of the tenant this document belongs to. |
| `data.createdAt` | string (ISO 8601) | Timestamp of when the document record was created. |
| `data.updatedAt` | string (ISO 8601) | Timestamp of when the document record was last updated. |

**Example Response Body:**

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Document created successfully",
  "data": {
    "id": 3,
    "file_name": "doc21",
    "file_size": null,
    "file_type": "unknown",
    "tenant_id": 2,
    "createdAt": "2026-04-25T11:42:15.789Z",
    "updatedAt": "2026-04-25T11:42:15.790Z"
  }
}

 ```

## Delete Document

Deletes a specific document by its ID. This endpoint permanently removes the document from the system.

---

### URL Structure

```
DELETE http://localhost:3000/documents/:id

 ```

The path parameter `3` in the example URL represents the **document ID** — replace it with the ID of the document you wish to delete.

| Path Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | number | The unique identifier of the document to delete |

---

### Request Details

- **Method:** `DELETE`
    
- **Request Body:** None required
    

---

### Response

#### 200 OK

Returned when the document is successfully deleted.

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Document deleted successfully"
}

 ```

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `success` | boolean | Indicates whether the deletion succeeded |
| `message` | string | A human-readable confirmation message |

#### Notes

Currently there is no authorization to delete docs. Will add later.

## Health Check

Checks the overall health and availability of the service and its dependent infrastructure components.

**Method:** `GET`
**URL:** `http://localhost:3001/health`

---

### Request

No request body or parameters required.

---

### Response

**Status:** `200 OK`

```json
{
  "status": "ok",
  "services": {
    "database": "up",
    "redis": "up",
    "elasticsearch": "up",
    "kafka": "up"
  }
}
```

#### Response Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `status` | `string` | Overall health status of the service. `"ok"` indicates the service is running normally. |
| `services` | `object` | A map of dependent services and their individual statuses. |
| `services.database` | `string` | Status of the primary database connection. `"up"` means the database is reachable and operational. |
| `services.redis` | `string` | Status of the Redis cache. `"up"` means Redis is reachable and operational. |
| `services.elasticsearch` | `string` | Status of the Elasticsearch instance. `"up"` means it is reachable and operational. |
| `services.kafka` | `string` | Status of the Kafka message broker. `"up"` means Kafka is reachable and operational. |

---

### Notes

- This endpoint is typically used by load balancers, monitoring tools, or orchestration systems (e.g., Kubernetes liveness/readiness probes) to verify that the service and all its dependencies are healthy.
- A `status` of `"ok"` with all services reporting `"up"` indicates a fully operational system.
- If any dependent service is unavailable, its value may change to `"down"` or similar, and the overall `status` may reflect a degraded or unhealthy state.
